Description: Standard calculator, to be used as a shell to make it look like an original.
A 2.4 inch touchscreen display to replace the original calculator screen.
Custom PCB that has pads that can be wired to the Pi instead of just hooking up to the OG calculator screen.
The main computer which is just powerful enough to run Linux and I can program it how I want, to act as a normal calculator and enter the secret menu. It has included wifi to constantly pull updates.
Input controller for the various row and column pads from the PCB, since I'd run out of pins on the Pi Zero 2 W.
Battery to power the entire calculator.

Item: 30 AWG PTFE Plastic Ultra Fine Wire
Description: Super thin wire to solder with.
An assortment of screws to use on the back of the calculator.
Insulating tape that can't melt to prevent short circuiting.
To charge the battery and safely connect it to the Pi.
Main power switch to turn it on and off.
